title.zh: 邊界之戰：德國選舉中的隱形壁壘
title.en: Which Border Matters Most? The Hidden Divides in German Elections

summary.zh:
你是否想過，為什麼同一個國家的選民，在邊界兩側的投票選擇會有如此巨大的差異？這篇發表於《政治地理學》的論文揭開了德國選舉背後的隱形壁壘。研究者並非將選區視為獨立單元，而是聚焦於鄰近選區之間的選舉差異——就像拆開拼圖時，真正決定圖像完整性的，往往是拼圖塊之間的接縫。

論文提出一個創新概念：用「選舉差異指數」來衡量不同行政邊界對投票行為的影響。結果發現，德國東西分裂時期的「內部分界線」（即前東德與西德邊界）造成的選票落差最為顯著。更有趣的是，國會議員選票受選區邊界影響較大，但政黨整體得票率卻與州、縣級行政邊界高度相關。這就像兩塊拼圖之間的裂縫，有的地方影響選民對候選人的偏好，有的地方則決定整體政黨支持率的走向。
summary.en:
Imagine Germany as a mosaic of voting districts, where each tile holds a different political story. But what if the real story lies not in the tiles themselves, but in the gaps between them? A groundbreaking study in *Political Geography* reveals how different types of administrative boundaries shape electoral outcomes—and why some borders matter more than others.

Rather than analyzing election results within territorial units like constituencies or states, the researchers measured the differences in voting behavior between neighboring units. Using a modified version of the Gallagher index (a standard tool for measuring electoral disproportionality), they found that the former East-West German border created the most significant electoral divide. Surprisingly, constituency boundaries matter most for candidate votes, but they are irrelevant for party votes. Instead, electoral differences in party vote shares are most pronounced at the borders of states, government districts, and counties. This distinction is like a fault line in the political landscape: some boundaries split votes between candidates, while others define the broader political identity of a region.

keyArticle.title: Which border matters most? An analysis of how different types of internal boundaries affect voting behavior
